title: apt/dpkg refuses: 'dpkg was interrupted, run dpkg --configure -a'
description_md:
A package operation was killed mid-way during a maintenance window. Now every `apt`/`dpkg` action refuses with `dpkg was interrupted, you must manually run 'dpkg --configure -a'`, leaving a package unpacked but not configured. SSH in as `trainee` (passwordless `sudo`), bring dpkg's database back to a consistent, fully-configured state.

You're done when no packages are left half-configured (`dpkg --audit` is clean).
